method_atom: An effective-action / variational free-energy analysis of a many-body system whose interactions are non-local, showing that the spatially uniform state becomes unstable and spontaneously breaks continuous translational symmetry, so the ordered ground state is a spatially periodic, modulated ('stripe') pattern rather than a homogeneous one.
object_atom: A self-interacting scalar field with a quartic (phi^4) coupling defined on a two-dimensional non-commutative space, whose non-locality stems from the non-commutative product of coordinates.
brief_reason: What transfers is the mechanism by which non-local interactions drive spontaneous breaking of translational symmetry into a modulated stripe phase, analyzed via effective-action techniques; the two-dimensional non-commutative quartic scalar field is the domain-specific object.
